Senior leadership is dedicated to employee wellbeing, and holds leaders accountable for practicing the leadership core values of the company. Great benefits. Great benefits and senior leadership cares about employees
Career growth, skillset development, multivendor collaboration, keeps your skills sharp Great place to start a career, but taxing

Lots of the best minds come to work at Ericsson and there are some truly wonderful people that cycle through. Those that stay are often the ones who find the right words that North American senior leadership likes to here. They like to hear they are great leaders, everything is great, and everything will be great. Late-stage company based on North American leadership perspective
good benefits hybrid work remote unstable too many leadership changes

Working together on these projects would allow us to contribute to the community positively while also fostering our own professional growth and teamwork skills. I believe that being active in such initiatives not only represents Ericsson's values but also enhances our work experience by connecting us with colleagues across different functions. Order Management

Team and Culture (Initially): My immediate team was exceptional, fostering a collaborative and supportive environment. Professional Growth: The role offered substantial opportunities for personal and career development, especially in the early days post-acquisition. Product and Innovation: Working with Cradlepoint's technology was a joy, and the product line was innovative, which made selling and supporting it rewar...

Adequate management; Company culture; Good compensation and benefits; Good work to life balance. Good company to work at.
Good benefits. Remote position. Immediate management has great communication Tower Technician
Great place to be for cutting edge wireless products and services. I feel like I make an impact to the industry and people's lives. Delivery Manager
Company is not as process/tool oriented as it should be to function as efficiently as possible. Solid company, great benefits
Too much internal focus, Salaries are not competitive + benefits, Innovation is not fast, Great Tech Company , Low Benefits, Too much internal focus

Company strategy does not align with rapidly changing ICT industry trends, organization is failing to adapt, appears to believe it can tell customers what they need/should buy. Executive leadership rotates through from job to job, very little infusion of new executive talent from within the organization or from outside. Excellent benefits, floundering strategy and entrenched executives

Was part of an acquisition and never felt fully integrated. Closed minds on previous ccompanies'ways of working even though were more automated. Large Corp that did not embrace remote work until the Pandemic
Stressful meetings, global teams, tight deadlines Great

Only Swedes get a promotion. Only people under 50 get a promotion. Even though the CEO repeatedly asked staff to speak-up, DONT. The top 3 leadership layers are Swedish, who resent the Santa Clara work force as "too expensive." Don't speak up. It'll get your terminated.
Nearly all leadership will be Sweedish, I wouldn't try for senior positions given that reality Great learning opportunity
- Not much of career path especially for tech jobs - A lot of old technical solution which does not give you much opportunity to learn new stuff Solution Architect
Coorporate with alot of politics involved to move forward and no room for career growth Stay Away
Need to be a good buddy with upper management to grow in the organization. Your performance is not appreciated and valued. Good option for professionals looking to retire soon.
Salaries are not competitive with the market Good Work Life balance

HORRIBLE culture of favoritism. This office epitomizes the 90 -10 rule. 10% of the staff or less does any of the work while the rest are allowed to show up late or not at all and do little to no work. This office needs a total makeover from staff and management. TOXIC culture that does not allow growth or upward movement. Instead of addressing problems, they bring in old staff that just continue to repeat the same w...
less predictable schedules and overall very slow paced. Not a paymaster amazing work life balance
Transparency is not good company wide, unrealistic expectations Work-life balance
Nothing specifically, but Ericsson is not a pay master. Great company - Best Work/Life balance

evidence.role: Current employee - Strategic Sourcing Category Manager
evidence.text: - IT tools. - Job rotation flexibility varies from a department to another. - Frequent changes between globalizing and localizing some entities. - While overall benefits are valuable, might not be the best paying in market. Diverse and Inclusive Culture
I can only speak for network build. You're likely to end up in a bad, toxic team. Work-Live balance is non-existent. Lots of managers are disconnected, toxic, rigid and arrogant. Circus

Management. Constant gaslighting. People get spread thin after countless layoffs and no backfill. Only offshoring. Constant praises, promotion promises that never mount to anything. Upper management thinks they are transparent. But, non stop lies at Q&A portion of all employ meeting Once great company to work for now toxic
Too much internal politics between organizations for credits and visibility. It leads to low morale on working level and impact customer satisfaction. Internal politics instead of customer focus
Lots of politics. If you thrive in political environment, this is the right place for you. If your worth is your skillset and good work ethics, apply elsewhere. Lots of politics
Poor communication budget is drawing a lot of cutbacks not much advancement opportunity Poor communication throughout company.

1. If you are a woman- RUN. They pay a lot of lip service about DEI and ERGs to cover up whatâs really lurking beneath. 2. If you are not Swedish you will not be promoted 3. Long hours for less than industry average pay 4. Promotions are not based on skill or merit, rather who will kiss the ring and stroke egos 5. Rapidly declining business case for NextG. Already long technology cycles exasperated mean more layof...

Too much process and governance. They are trying to eliminate some of the hassle, but they keep placing the wrong people in the wrong executive roles. Because of this, they are not able to simplify and increase their bottom line. A layoff can come at any time without any warning, if the corporate office decides to make changes on the fly. They want to change, but they keep shooting themselves in the foot.
Global leadership not in touch with local challenges Excellent Teamwork
Lots of cost savings recently, too many restructuring. Needs to pick up with competition
